title: Clinical apps
desc: Structured input → grounded LLM reasoning → your review. The result appears in the chat, and each tool call asks for your approval.
genFailed: AI generation failed; loaded a built-in sample instead.

triage: Chest pain with cold sweats, radiating to the left arm
High fever with chills, mildly altered consciousness, recent UTI
Sudden dyspnea and wheezing, history of asthma
soap: 60F, epigastric pain for 3h, nausea, no fever, soft abdomen, positive Murphy's sign, suspected gallstones
25M, asthma history, dyspnea for 2h, wheezing, SpO2 90%, partial improvement after inhaler
30M, inverted left ankle during sport, lateral malleolus swelling and pain, able to bear weight, no obvious deformity

triage.hint: Enter the chief complaint and vital signs; the LLM assesses them and suggests a TTAS level. The triage nurse makes the final call.
triage.complaintPlaceholder: e.g. chest pain with cold sweats
triage.prompt: Patient chief complaint: {{complaint}}; vital signs: {{vitals}}. First assess the vital signs and flag any critical red flags, then suggest a TTAS triage level with your reasoning.

soap.hint: Enter exam keywords; the LLM expands them into a SOAP note draft. The physician reviews and signs off.
soap.keywordsPlaceholder: e.g. 60F, epigastric pain 3h, nausea, no fever, soft abdomen, suspected gallstones
soap.prompt: Using the SOAP format, expand the following emergency-department exam keywords into a note draft (note type: {{noteType}}): {{keywords}}. Only expand on what is provided; mark gaps as 'to be completed'.
